data.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 8937 "Producer↔consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 coverage finding; no uncovered parent acceptance/success criterion remains across the roster.

8937 is the KEYSTONE. It owns more parent criteria than any sibling — the SSOT schema, the fetcher/differ schema-validation tests, the one production-path round-trip contract test, AND (explicitly named in the parent AC) the documented anti-change-detector guardrail.

OWNED — FULLY covered by THIS child:
- Parent AC "A single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema module exists as the canonical shape" → 8937 AC1 (`_snapshot_schema.py` TypedDict + JSON Schema as SSOT, covering every field the differs read: summary/description(ADF)/status/priority/assignee/labels/comment(nested)/issuelinks/parent). FULLY.
- Parent AC "A test validates the fetcher's snapshot output against that schema" → 8937 AC2 (validates `fetcher.fetch_snapshot` output vs the schema; designed to FAIL on the pre-fix conditions — issuelinks absent; comment under the wrong key). FULLY.
- Parent AC "A test validates each differ's snapshot reads against that schema" → 8937 AC1 (schema covers every field the differs read) + AC3 (the round-trip drives inbound_differ + outbound_differ reading the schema-validated snapshot, asserting each consumer-depended key/shape is produced & read correctly). FULLY at roster level.
- Parent AC "One round-trip contract test exercises fetcher → snapshot → differs → applier through the production path, using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads" → 8937 AC3 (the joint producer→consumer round-trip through the PRODUCTION path, FakeAcliClient → fetch_snapshot → differs → applier) + fe57 (the "fixtures derived from real Jira payloads" half; 8937 declares depends_on fe57 and scopes fixture capture OUT). FULLY as a pairing (test → 8937; fixtures → fe57).
- Parent AC "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" → 8937 AC5 + description ("Owns the epic's guardrail artifact (resolves the roster-level gap): commit a documented anti-change-detector guardrail … a short docs/ note (ADR-style) … New tests reference it"; file_impact docs/adr/0004-reconciler-snapshot-contract.md). The parent AC VERBATIM names its owner ("owned by story B" = THIS child). FULLY owned here.

CONTRIBUTED:
- Parent SC "Producer (fetcher + enrichment) and consumers (differs/appliers) cannot drift on snapshot shape without a test failing" → 8937 OWNS the keystone half (schema SSOT pins the shape both sides target; the joint round-trip jointly exercises producer+consumer so a key/shape divergence — the exact bug-0ee6/3f04 failure — fails a test immediately). Complemented by fe57 (honest fixtures/fake foundation) and 59ef (verified-fake fidelity). CONTRIBUTED (keystone half).
- Parent SC "New tests assert behavior/contract (semantic round-trip, structure/type); the suite stays lean; zero change-detector tests added" → 8937 AC4 (all assertions semantic — keys/types/round-trip — never whole-blob golden equality or interaction/mock-call-count; Dredd/Gavel structure-not-value posture) + AC5 (the committed guardrail records the rules new tests follow). CONTRIBUTED (and 8937 is the documented governing-artifact owner for this posture).

DELEGATION — parent criteria THIS child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; 8937's declared OUT items (capturing fixtures → story A/fe57; collapsing appliers → story D/33d0; telemetry → story E/2359) are consistent with the roster:
- AC "Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path (no parallel applier remains)" + AC "The retired outbound applier path is deleted" + SC "Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path" → 33d0 (closed; AC1 + parity-before-delete gate).
- AC "Outbound sub-op telemetry (links_applied/comments_applied/labels_applied) surfaced in the structured batch outcome" + AC "computed > 0 && applied == 0 fails loudly, after the warn-first rollout" + SC "A silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary instead of passing green" → 2359 (closed; AC1/AC2/AC3, depends_on 33d0).
- AC "Bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip is asserted hermetically through the production path" + AC "Every probe assertion that encodes a current bug as expected is converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes" + SC "Bidirectional round-trips … asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" → 822a (closed; AC1/AC2/AC3, depends_on fe57).
- Real-payload fixtures + FakeAcliClient foundation (the "fixtures derived from real Jira payloads" half of 8937 AC3, and the verified-fake fidelity that keeps it honest) → fe57 + 59ef.

ANTI-FP — the historically recurring "Parent AC6 documented anti-change-detector guardrail only PARTIALLY covered — no sibling produces a governing artifact / governs new tests" finding (raised in early roster audits) is a FALSE POSITIVE and stays WITHDRAWN, and it is especially inapplicable on THIS pairing: the parent AC reads verbatim "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" — it explicitly names THIS child (story B = 8937) as the owner, and 8937 delivers it (description + AC5 + committed docs/adr/0004-reconciler-snapshot-contract.md). Under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a NAMED consumer counts), this is FULLY covered. The same false positive has already been withdrawn across siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/fe57.

4-bucket summary for this pairing:
- FULLY (OWNED by THIS child): parent AC "SSOT snapshot-entry schema"; parent AC "fetcher snapshot validated vs schema"; parent AC "each differ's reads validated vs schema"; parent AC "round-trip contract test through the production path" (test half — fixtures half → fe57); parent AC "documented anti-change-detector guardrail (owned by story B)".
- CONTRIBUTED (keystone half): parent SC "producer/consumers cannot drift on snapshot shape"; parent SC "semantic assertions / lean suite / zero change-detector".
- COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING: applier collapse + retired-path deletion + production-path exercise (33d0); sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary (2359); bidirectional hermetic round-trip + probe de-encoding (822a); real-payload fixtures + FakeAcliClient foundation (fe57); verified-fake fidelity (59ef).
- STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE: parent AC "All six child stories are closed; the full reconciler suite is green."
- PARTIALLY / UNCOVERED: none.

No G3 coverage finding for this pairing. The union of children covers every parent acceptance and success criterion; THIS child fully delivers its (large) owned share — the canonical snapshot-entry schema, the fetcher/differ schema-validation tests, the single producer→consumer round-trip contract through the production path, and the documented anti-change-detector guardrail the parent AC explicitly assigns to story B — and no parent criterion is left uncovered by, or solely-and-uncovered on account of, this child.
